title: Photography
text:
As a wizard and scholar of Middle-earth, I have been studying the magic of the natural world for centuries. Through my self-portraits, I aim to capture the essence of my own being and reflect on my own journey through time. Each photograph is a reflection of my own experiences and emotions. Through my photography, I hope to offer a glimpse into my life as a scholar and adventurer, and inspire others to reflect on their own journeys through the world.

title: Research
text:
Zewei Kong, P. A. Parker, & S. H. Holan (in preparation, 2024)
There is currently no unified, efficient, and simple framework to handle mixed-type data (Gaussian and binomial). This methodological gap is the primary motivation for our work. Instead of building upon the multivariate Fay-Herriot framework, we propose a new joint model that utilizes a similar strategy of setting the variance as known. By leveraging the Gibbs sampler, this approach simplifies computations and enhances overall estimation accuracy for dependent Gaussian and binomial outcomes. (Note: While programs like SAIPE demonstrate the importance of tracking income and poverty, the core driver of this study is advancing the statistical modeling framework itself.)



Zewei Kong, J. Bradley, P. A. Parker, & S. H. Holan (in preparation, 2025)
Focused on unit-level dependent responses, we proposed a new model employing the Gibbs sampler to enhance performance and computational efficiency. The approach implements pseudo-likelihood techniques within a Bayesian framework, demonstrating improved performance on large-scale datasets such as the ACS Public Use Microdata Sample for California, analyzing over 390,000 unit records.

title: Projects
text:
arXiv Preprint | Code
Over the centuries, I have devoted countless hours to deciphering and translating the ancient scripts and dialects of the various peoples of Middle-earth. My goal is to gain a deeper understanding of the cultures and histories of these peoples by studying their languages. Currently, I am working on a monograph that explores the linguistic roots of the Elvish languages. Through extensive research and analysis, I hope to shed light on the connections between the different dialects of Elvish and their origins. This project has been particularly challenging, as Elvish is a complex and nuanced language, but I am determined to see it through to completion.

title: Projects
section: The History of the War of the Ring
text:
The History of the War of the Ring

arXiv Preprint | Code
I am creating a comprehensive and detailed history of the conflict that goes beyond the surface-level events. By gathering information from a variety of sources, including my own memories, written accounts, and oral histories, I hope to shed new light on this important period in Middle-earth’s history and provide valuable insights into the motivations and actions of the various players involved.

title: Welcome to Zewei’s Web
text:
Github
  
  
    
     LinkedIn
  
  
    
     Email
  




Welcome to Zewei’s Web
Hello! I am Zewei Kong, a Ph.D. Candidate at the University of Missouri. My work bridges the gap between rigorous statistical methodology and practical, data-driven application development.
My primary research interests lie in Bayesian statistics and data augmentation techniques. Currently, my research focuses on developing a new joint model designed to handle mixed-type data (Gaussian and binomial). The core motivation behind this work is to provide a unified, efficient, and simple framework where one currently does not exist.
Beyond theoretical research, I am highly passionate about building tools that make data actionable. I regularly use R and Python in VS Code to develop applications. Some of my recent software projects include TraffIQ and Melonu.
Whether it is discussing advanced Bayesian models, app development, or potential postdoctoral research opportunities, please feel free to contact me. I am always open to connecting with fellow researchers and developers.

View My CV
